preview_text:
The model heavily favors Croatia, assigning them a 78.5% win probability and viewing Ghana as a substantial underdog at 2.9%. This 75-percentage-point gap reflects the model's assessment that Croatia is the clear favorite, though a Ghana upset remains within the realm of possibility rather than negligible.

The win-draw-loss spread suggests a tilted but not entirely one-sided contest. Croatia's commanding win probability coexists with an 18.7% draw chance, indicating the model sees meaningful risk of a stalemate. Ghana's single-digit win probability implies the model anticipates limited paths to victory, though draws occur often enough in group play that neither side can be ruled out of advancing.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge sharply. Books and prediction markets grant Ghana roughly 17% odds—six to seven times higher than the model's 2.9%—and assign draw probability near 29%, compared to the model's 18.7%. The dashboard offers no judgment on which forecast is correct. The gap warrants attention; it suggests either the model's Elo inputs or recency assumptions diverge materially from market expectations.
